-{"id":"asupersync-36m6p","title":"[RAPTORQ-RFC6330][E5] Offline Kernel Superoptimization + Architecture Profile Packs","description":"Build an offline kernel superoptimization workflow that explores tile/unroll/prefetch/fusion variants for GF256 superkernels, emits architecture-specific profile packs, and installs deterministic runtime dispatch tables selecting the best validated variant per CPU feature set.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Offline tuner generates candidate GF256 kernel variants (tile size, unroll, prefetch distance, fusion shape) and benchmarks them on representative workloads.
2. Selected variants are emitted as deterministic architecture profile packs (e.g., AVX2 class, NEON class, scalar class) with explicit metadata and versioning.
3. Runtime dispatch chooses profile-pack variant deterministically from stable feature detection and always preserves scalar conservative fallback.
4. End-to-end decode/encode throughput and p95/p99 latency improve materially versus static E4 baseline on supported architectures.
5. Unit + E2E Validation Requirement: deterministic unit tests and deterministic E2E scenarios validate bit-exactness and stable dispatch across profile packs.
6. Structured Logging Requirement: logs include architecture class, selected profile id, rejected candidates, fallback reason, and replay pointer.
7. Reproducibility Requirement: every tuning result is reproducible from pinned corpus, environment metadata, and command bundle.","notes":"[2026-02-22 TurquoiseBridge] Taking over E5 for fresh comparator evidence pass to resolve Track-E unblock uncertainty.

Canonical scope clarification (2026-02-22):
- E5 is the canonical implementation/tuning bead for offline superoptimization, profile-pack construction, and deterministic runtime dispatch selection.
- E5 must emit artifact bundles consumable by E3 validation and Track-G governance gates.
- E5 should not absorb final independent statistical sign-off responsibilities (owned by E3) or fusion gate semantics ownership (owned by 2ncba.6).

 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.15","title":"[FABRIC][P15.15] Privacy-budgeted metadata surfaces","description":"Apply calibrated differential-privacy noise to exported metadata summaries. File: src/messaging/privacy.rs

 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.18","title":"[FABRIC][P15.18] Speculative subject-cell execution","description":"Tentative publish with deterministic rollback for low-conflict cells. File: src/messaging/fabric.rs (speculative portion)

- For low-conflict subject cells and service classes that permit it:
  1. Origin issues tentative publish backed by tentative obligation
  2. Control capsule sequences in background
  3. If confirmed: obligation commits, tentative path becomes final
  4. If conflict: tentative obligation aborts deterministically, corrected outcome surfaced (no illegal intermediate state exposed to consumers)
- Bounded by: service class, cell-local conflict histograms, explicit kill switches
- Verified under same replay/oracle machinery as cancellation and redelivery races
- NOT generic 'superposed execution' — narrowly justified latency optimization with explicit rollback
- Tests: successful speculative publish (confirm), conflict rollback (abort), kill switch, conflict histogram tracking, replay verification","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:33:46.636781540Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:23:56.864886486Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["advanced","fabric","speculative"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.18","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:33:46.636781540Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2538,"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.18","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Speculative execution guardrails: (1) Bounded by service class, (2) Bounded by cell-local conflict histograms — auto-disable above threshold, (3) Bounded by explicit operator kill switches, (4) Verified under same replay/oracle machinery, (5) Tentative obligations are distinct type, (6) Consumers NEVER see tentative results, (7) Rollback is deterministic. NOT generic superposed execution — narrowly justified latency optimization.","created_at":"2026-03-18T06:10:54Z"},{"id":2541,"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.18","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Speculative execution guardrails (CRITICAL — must be enforced): This feature is NOT generic 'superposed execution.' It is a narrowly justified latency optimization with EXPLICIT rollback semantics. Guardrails: (1) Bounded by service class — only permitted for classes that explicitly allow it. (2) Bounded by cell-local conflict histograms — if conflict rate exceeds threshold, speculation is disabled for that cell. (3) Bounded by explicit kill switches — operator can disable per-class or per-cell. (4) Verified under the SAME replay and oracle machinery as cancellation and redelivery races — speculation must not introduce behaviors that escape the existing verification framework. (5) Tentative obligations are a distinct obligation type that cannot be confused with committed obligations. (6) Consumer-visible state NEVER includes tentative results — consumers see only committed state. (7) Rollback is deterministic — same conflict always produces the same rollback outcome. Do NOT claim this is universally beneficial. It is a targeted optimization for specific workload patterns.","created_at":"2026-03-18T06:23:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.19","title":"[FABRIC][P15.19] Subject-native multi-tenant namespace kernel","description":"NATS accounts/imports/exports already look like an OS namespace model. Asupersync should lean into this analogy. Subjects become:
- **Process mailboxes**: each actor/service addressable via subject
- **Service names**: service discovery via subject registration
- **Control channels**: per-service and per-tenant control subjects
- **Durable stream capture rules**: stream capture policy tied to subject namespace
- **Trust boundaries**: import/export morphisms define security perimeters
- **Observability feeds**: per-tenant and per-service telemetry subjects
The runtime starts to look like a "secure, replayable, distributed namespace kernel."
This is NOT about pretending to solve full CPU/memory/storage isolation — it is the namespace analogy applied to subject-based addressing. The capability model (Cx + tokens) provides the authority enforcement. The morphism system provides the namespace mapping.
- File: integrated across src/messaging/subject.rs, capability/, and control.rs
- Tests: multi-tenant namespace setup, cross-tenant isolation, namespace-as-service-discovery, namespace-as-trust-boundary","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T16:43:47.963610169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T16:43:47.963610169Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["advanced","fabric","multi-tenant","namespace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16.19","depends_on_id":"asupersync-8w83i.16","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T16:43:47.963610169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
